commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Gmail watch Pub/Sub pull consumer command, generated command docs, focused tests, and a new Google Cloud Pub/Sub dependency.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection, `GmailWatchPullCmd.Run` never checks `flags.DryRun` before `receiver.Receive`, and the pull handler can Ack/Nack messages and update state.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A contributor or maintainer needs to fix the dry-run blocker and provide live Gmail/Pub/Sub proof; this is not a safe automated repair lane while proof is missing.

target.title: feat(gmail): add Pub/Sub pull watch consumer
target.body:
# Human-written request

i think you should now start drafting implementation for each of the tools in worktrees and then opening draft PRs against the repos, at least for gog and openclaw. can you do that using [$session-goal-writer](/nix/store/hcks99ivrjhg2pg09lc97cqhd2ixh1w1-source/skills/session-goal-writer/SKILL.md) ?

# Draft scope

RFC: openclaw/rfcs#8
Companion OpenClaw PR: openclaw/openclaw#90723

This draft adds the no-inbound Gmail notification runtime for the RFC: `gog gmail settings watch pull`, plus the hidden compatibility path `gog gmail watch pull`.

# What changed

- Adds a Pub/Sub pull consumer for Gmail watch notifications.
- Reuses the existing Gmail watch processor for push and pull so account filtering, stale history handling, history fetches, hook payloads, and hook failure behavior stay aligned.
- Requires a full Pub/Sub subscription resource path: `projects/<project>/subscriptions/<subscription>`.
- Requires an explicit or stored hook target before consuming messages, so the command does not silently drain a subscription with nowhere to deliver notifications.
- Uses explicit terminal ack/nack behavior for invalid messages, wrong-account messages, hook failures, Gmail failures, and rate-limit circuit failures.
- Regenerates command docs for the new command.

# Compatibility

- Existing `gog gmail watch serve` push behavior remains supported.
- This does not change Gmail watch registration behavior or cloud resource setup.
- This does not remove shared-token push.

# Testing strategy

Already run in this draft:

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go test ./internal/cmd -run 'GmailWatch'`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command go build -o /tmp/gog-gmail-pull ./cmd/gog`
- `/tmp/gog-gmail-pull gmail settings watch pull --help`
- `/tmp/gog-gmail-pull gmail watch pull --help`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command make docs-commands`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go --command make fmt-check`
- `git diff --check`

Deterministic coverage added here:

- fake Pub/Sub receiver proves the command creates a receiver, consumes one-at-a-time, uses stored hook settings, and closes cleanly;
- invalid Pub/Sub payload and wrong-account notifications ack terminally;
- hook failure acks after recording `http_error`, matching the existing no-replay-storm behavior;
- Gmail/history failures nack so Pub/Sub can redeliver;
- missing subscription and missing hook fail before consuming.

Agent-runnable next proof, no Josh action required once a maintainer runner has normal Go tooling:

- `make ci` on a clean Linux runner;
- `go test ./...` to catch dependency and package-level fallout outside `internal/cmd`;
- optional clean-package smoke: build `gog`, run `gog gmail settings watch pull --help`, and confirm `gog gmail watch serve --help` still exists.

Live Google proof still needed before calling this production-ready:

1. Use a disposable Gmail account and GCP project with Gmail API and Pub/Sub enabled.
2. Create a topic and pull subscription.
3. Register a Gmail watch for the topic.
4. Start a local fake hook server.
5. Run `gog gmail settings watch pull --account <account> --subscription projects/<project>/subscriptions/<subscription> --hook-url <fake-hook> --hook-token <token>` with subscriber credentials.
6. Send one test email to the disposable mailbox.
7. Verify the worker pulls the Pub/Sub notification, fetches Gmail history, posts one hook payload, and acks only after terminal handling.

# Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gmail Pub/Sub can now be consumed with pull semantics instead of requiring Google to call an inbound HTTP endpoint.

Real environment tested: local macOS worktree with Go 1.26 from Nix; no live Google resources were mutated.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: see the commands in `Testing strategy` above.

Evidence after fix: focused GmailWatch tests pass; the new command builds and prints help; generated docs include `gog gmail settings watch pull`.

Observed result after fix: the fake receiver tests prove terminal ack/nack policy and hook delivery behavior without live Google credentials.

What was not tested: real Gmail watch registration, real Pub/Sub pull, real subscriber credentials, and real mailbox delivery.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123
